source.title: How to use ChatGPT Work for everyday tasks

userProblem.en: What information should you give an agent before handing off real work?
userProblem.zh: 把真实任务交给 Agent 前，需要写清哪些信息？

centralThesis.en: Do not start from a blank prompt. Package a concrete artifact, approved source materials, constraints, review expectations, destination format, and handoff state so the agent can return a review-ready first version.
centralThesis.zh: 不要从空白提示词开始。把具体产物、批准的源材料、约束、审阅预期、交付格式和交接状态打包，让 Agent 返回一份可审阅的第一版。

P0: Start from a concrete artifact rather than a vague activity.
Provide the real work materials that can change the result.
Make constraints, review expectations, and destination format observable.
Return a first usable version with evidence, open questions, and a clear handoff.
P1: Define authority order and freshness for source materials.
Separate hard constraints from preferences and define permissions and stop conditions.
Use acceptance criteria and inspectable evidence before consequential approval.
Maintain a six-field Agent Task Brief as the task changes.
P2: Use bounded examples for weekly operating briefs, retention analysis, launch packages, and publishing handoffs.
Preserve the source boundary: examples explain the method and are not presented as measured OpenAI outcomes.
P3: Product navigation, webinar details, and exhaustive use-case listings are compressed because they do not change the handoff method.

factBoundaries: The source directly supports starting from existing work materials, asking for a first usable artifact, and including source materials, constraints, review expectations, and destination format.
The six-field brief, authority map, permission ladder, stop conditions, and review template are explanatory tools created for this video.
No illustrative example is presented as an OpenAI customer case study or measured result.

reusableArtifact.name: Agent Task Brief / Agent 任务委托单
reusableArtifact.purpose: Package the smallest complete assignment that can produce and verify a review-ready first artifact.
fields: outcome artifact, user, supported decision, and usable-state definition
approved source map, authority order, freshness, and known gaps
hard constraints, preferences, permissions, prohibited actions, and stop conditions
acceptance criteria, required evidence, reviewers, and approval gates
destination structure, schema, dimensions, filenames, and output location
completed work, unresolved questions, checked evidence, and next allowed action

chapters.purpose: Replace the blank prompt with the smallest complete work package
Define the reviewable deliverable and supported decision
Make limits, permissions, and stop conditions checkable
Define acceptance criteria and inspectable evidence
Specify the container, structure, location, and state
Restate the method and hand off the fixed CTA

sharedVisualSemantics.humanRole: defines the artifact, approves sources and limits, judges evidence, and owns consequential decisions
sharedVisualSemantics.agentRole: receives the bounded package, produces the first usable artifact, exposes uncertainty, preserves state, and returns evidence
compositionIntent: comparison for blank prompt versus real work
grid for the four observable inputs
process paths for source authority, stop conditions, review, and handoff
oversized task brief for the reusable artifact

localeRule: English and Chinese share thesis, priority, chapter order, fact boundaries, character tasks, and reusable artifact; narration, VTT, timing map, and animation timings are compiled independently.
